Step 1: Analyze the statement to identify the type of logical fallacy.
The statement "How could I possibly have time to wash the dishes when so much carbon dioxide is being spewed into the atmosphere on a daily basis?" implies that because the speaker has so much work (washing dishes) and so much pollution (carbon dioxide emissions), they cannot possibly have the time to do both.

Step 2: Identify the logical fallacy.
The fallacy in this statement is **overgeneralization**. Overgeneralization occurs when someone generalizes a specific situation or experience to apply to all similar situations or experiences, or to all people
